zh.Aldingham Manor, Lancashire, England: 英國藍卡爾阿爾迪加姆
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Theobald le Fleming於1120年出生在英國藍卡爾阿爾迪加姆時，他的父親Michael le Fleming35歲，母親Alice Stuteville32歲。他於1150年之前與Kerdale De Moravia在蘇格蘭結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1193年在蘇格蘭米德洛迪安苗斯頓享年73歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭米德洛迪安苗斯頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Theobald le Fleming于1120年出生在英国藍卡爾阿爾迪加姆时，他的父亲Michael le Fleming35岁，母亲Alice Stuteville32岁。他于1150年之前与Kerdale De Moravia在苏格兰结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1193年在苏格兰米德洛迪安苗斯頓享年73岁，埋葬在苏格兰米德洛迪安苗斯頓。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Fleming
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: ethnic name for someone from Flanders, from Middle English fleming. The word reflects a Norman French form of Old French flamanc ‘Fleming’, from the stem flam- + the ancient Germanic suffix -ing. In the Middle Ages there was considerable commercial intercourse between England and the Netherlands, particularly in the wool trade, and many Flemish weavers and dyers settled in England. This surname is also common in south and east Scotland and in Ireland, where it is sometimes found in the Gaelicized form Pléamonn.
German: variant of   Flemming  , cognate with 1 above.

variants.name: Flemings
meanings: English: plural form of   Fleming  .
Americanized form of Flemish Vleminckx: patronymic from Vleminck, an ethnic name for someone from Flanders, Middle Dutch vleminc.

variants.name: Flanders
meanings: English: ethnic name from Middle English Fla(u)ndres ‘Flanders’, denoting someone from Flanders, a historical region covering parts of modern-day Belgium, France, and the Netherlands. Compare Fleming .

variants.name: Fleeman
meanings: English: variant of   Fleming  .

variants.name: Flemming
meanings: German: ethnic name for someone from Flanders, Middle High German vlaeminc. People from Flanders spread throughout Germany, as well as England, in the Middle Ages as clothmakers and dyers.
English: variant of   Fleming  .

variants.name: Flamenco
meanings: Spanish: ethnic name from flamenco ‘Fleming, Flemish person’ (see Fleming ).

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Old Norse, originally a byname meaning ‘bride, wife’; in mythology the name of Thor's golden-haired wife.

nameMeanings.name: Theobald
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from þeud ‘people, race’ + bald ‘bold, brave’. The first element was altered under the influence of Greek theos ‘god’. This name was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Theobald le Fleming was born in 1120, in Aldingham, Lancashire, England, his father, Michael le Fleming, was 35 and his mother, Alice Stuteville, was 32. He married Kerdale De Moravia before 1150, in Scotland. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1193, in Hermiston, Midlothian, Scotland, at the age of 73, and was buried in Hermiston, Midlothian, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Theobald le Fleming est né en 1120, à Aldingham, Lancashire, Angleterre, son père, Michael le Fleming, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Alice Stuteville, avait 32 ans. Il a épousé Kerdale De Moravia avant 1150, à Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1193, à Hermiston, Midlothian, Écosse, à l’âge de 73 ans, et a été enterré à Hermiston, Midlothian, Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Theobald le Fleming 1120 in Aldingham, Lancashire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Michael le Fleming 35 und seine Mutter Alice Stuteville 32. Er hat vor 1150 in Scotland Kerdale De Moravia geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1193 im Alter von 73 Jahren in Hermiston, Midlothian, Scotland und wurde in Hermiston, Midlothian, Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Theobald le Fleming è nato il nel 1120 nel Aldingham, Lancashire, England, il suo padre, Michael le Fleming, aveva 35 anni e il suo madre, Alice Stuteville, aveva 32 anni. Lui sposato Kerdale De Moravia prima del 1150, nel Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1193, nel Hermiston, Midlothian, Scotland, all'età di 73 anni, e fu sepolto a Hermiston, Midlothian, Scotland.

ja.Aldingham Manor, Lancashire, England: イギリスランカシュアイアアルディンガム
ja.lifeSummary: Sir Theobald le Flemingが1120年にイギリスランカシュアイアアルディンガムで生まれた時，父Michael le Flemingは35歳，母Alice Stutevilleは32歳であった。1150年以前にスコットランドでKerdale De Moraviaと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1193年にスコットランドミドロスアンハーミストンで73歳で死去し，スコットランドミドロスアンハーミストンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Theobald le Fleming(이)가 1120년 영국 랭카실르 알딩함에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Michael le Fleming(은)는 35세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice Stuteville(은)는 32세였다. 그는 1150년 이전 스컷랜드에서 Kerdale De Moravia(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1193년 스컷랜드 미들로스이언 허미스턴에서 73세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 미들로스이언 허미스턴에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Theobald le Fleming nasceu em 1120, em Aldingham, Lancashire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Michael le Fleming, tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, Alice Stuteville, tinha 32 anos. Ele casou-se com Kerdale De Moravia antes 1150, em Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 1193, em Hermiston, Midlothian, Scotland, com 73 anos, e foi sepultado em Hermiston, Midlothian, Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Theobald le Fleming родился в 1120 г. в местности алдингхам, ланкашир, Англия, его отцу по имени Michael le Fleming было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice Stuteville, 32. Он женился на женщине по имени Kerdale De Moravia перед 1150 г. в местности Шотландия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1193 г. в местности хирмистон, мидлотян, Шотландия в возрасте 73 лет и был похоронен в местности хирмистон, мидлотян, Шотландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Theobald le Fleming nació en 1120, en Aldingham, Lancashire, Inglaterra, su papá, Michael le Fleming, tenía 35 años y su mamá, Alice Stuteville, tenía 32 años. Se casó con Kerdale De Moravia antes de 1150, en Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1193, en Hermiston, Midlothian, Scotland, a la edad de 73 años, y fue sepultado en Hermiston, Midlothian, Scotland.
